title: Add null check before accessing user.email
description: The notification sender was crashing with AttributeError when user was None. Added a guard clause to return early if the user object is missing.
diff:
diff --git a/notifications/sender.py b/notifications/sender.py
index 4e5f6a7..b8c9d0e 100644
--- a/notifications/sender.py
+++ b/notifications/sender.py
@@ -10,6 +10,9 @@ def send_notification(user: User | None, message: str) -> bool:
     '''Send an email notification to the given user.
 
     Returns True if the notification was sent successfully.
     '''
+    if user is None:
+        logger.warning("Cannot send notification: user is None")
+        return False
+
     email = user.email
     if not email:
         logger.warning("User %s has no email address", user.id)
comments: julia: This was causing a prod crash on the nightly batch job. Good fix.
author: lily
